Drop Curve in Softball: A Comprehensive Guide ============================================== Introduction ------------ A drop curve is a type of pitch used in softball that moves downward as it approaches home plate, making it difficult for batters to hit. This guide will walk you through the steps necessary to master this technique. It requires practice and patience but can be an effective weapon when facing opposing batters. Step 1: Understanding the Basics --------------------------------- The first step towards throwing a successful drop curve involves understanding its mechanics. The spin applied causes the ball to move downwards due to gravity while maintaining speed, creating deception for the batter who expects a rising fastball or flat pitches like changeups and curves. Key Tip: Remember, just like any other pitch, timing and precision are crucial with a drop curve. Step 2: Grip Positioning ------------------------ Gripping the ball correctly sets the foundation for your pitch's movement and trajectory. Hold the baseball (yes, we start practicing with a regular baseball) across the seams diagonally, forming what looks like a 'V'. Your index finger and middle finger should rest over these seams at their highest point. Keep your thumb directly underneath them, supporting the bottom part of the ball. Guideline: Make sure there's space between your fingers; this allows proper spinning action during release. Step 3: Spin Direction --------------------- To get that signature dropping motion, impart clockwise rotation if you're right-handed or counterclockwise if left-handed. Imagine drawing tiny circles with your fingertips after releasing the ball - this visualization can help ensure correct spin direction. Key Tip: Focus on rotating only your wrist rather than using your entire arm. Too much body English might disrupt accuracy and control. Step 4: Arm Motion & Release Point ---------------------------------- Your throw starts from behind your head, similar to a fastball motion. However, instead of snapping your wrist straight forward upon release, twist it slightly outwards so the backspin turns into our intended tumbling descent. Timing is critical here because late adjustments could result in unwanted spins causing erratic movements. Guideline: Practice slow, deliberate motions until you feel comfortable with the twisting motion before gradually increasing speed. Step 5: Transitioning to Softball ----------------------------------- Once confident with a regulation baseball, transition slowly to a softball. Due to size differences, reposition your grip further apart along the stitches without changing the basic hold position explained earlier. Gradually adapt your muscle memory to accommodate the new weight distribution. Key Tip: Don't rush this process! Patience leads to consistency which eventually translates into effectiveness against opponents. Step 6: Fine Tuning & Consistency Building ------------------------------------------- Like all skills, perfecting the drop curve takes consistent effort. Regularly schedule practice sessions focusing solely on refining this pitch. Work closely with coaches or trainers to identify areas needing improvement and track progress over time. Guideline: Film yourself pitching to analyze form and spot inconsistencies overlooked during real-time observation. Conclusion ---------- Throwing a devastating drop curve adds versatility to your arsenal, keeping batters guessing throughout games. By following these steps meticulously and committing to continuous learning, even novice players stand a chance to develop this advanced skill set. Happy Pitching! Do you want to read this last page? When this happened, anti-slavery Free-Staters from New England and pro-slavery people from Missouri quickly came to Kansas. This process will help you understand the competition, demand, pricing strategies, and marketing tactics used by other sellers. By thoroughly evaluating the online market, you can make informed decisions about which products to sell, how to price them, and how to effectively market them to potential customers. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to evaluate the online market of a product: ### Step 1: Identify Your Target Audience and Niche Before diving into researching specific products, it's important to identify your target audience and niche. Understanding who your ideal customer is and what they are looking for will help guide your research and ensure that you focus on products that have a high likelihood of success. Key questions to ask yourself include: * Who is my target customer? Consider demographics such as age, gender, income level, location, interests, and pain points. * What problem does my product solve for this customer? How does it improve their life or meet a need they have? * What keywords would this customer use when searching for a solution like mine? Once you have a clear understanding of your target audience and niche, you can move on to researching specific products within that space. ### Step 2: Research Popular Products in Your Niche Start by identifying popular products within your niche. You can do this by conducting keyword research using tools like Google Trends, SEMrush, or Ahrefs. These tools allow you to see which search terms related to your niche are most frequently used by users. From there, you can visit e-commerce websites like Amazon, eBay, and AliExpress to see which products rank highest for those keywords. When analyzing these products, pay attention to factors such as: * Price point: Is the product competitively priced compared to similar offerings? * Reviews and ratings: Are customers generally satisfied with the product, or do negative reviews indicate areas for improvement? * Sales volume: High sales volumes may indicate strong demand for the product. * Features and benefits: Take note of any unique features or benefits that set the product apart from competitors. By examining successful products within your niche, you can gain valuable insights into what works well and what doesn't. Use this information to inform your own product development and marketing strategy. ### Step 3: Analyze Competitor Strategies The first inks were carbon inks, made from soot, which is 80% carbon, water and gum arabic. It's completely normal to have crushes and want to express your feelings to that special someone. However, asking a boy out can feel intimidating, especially if you've never done it before. This guide will provide you with clear steps, helpful tips, and crucial guidelines to help you ask a boy out confidently and appropriately. Keep in mind that everyone develops at their own pace, so there is no need to rush into anything. Take your time and move forward when you feel ready. ### Key Tips Before Getting Started * Respect his feelings and personal space. If he says 'no,' understand that it doesn't reflect negatively on you; he may simply not be interested or ready for a relationship. * Be yourself! Authenticity goes a long way in any interaction, and being genuine will make both of you more comfortable. * Practice good communication skills by listening actively, speaking clearly, and maintaining eye contact. These skills will serve you well throughout life, including during this potentially nerve-wracking moment. " How to Be a Health Unit Coordinator A health unit coordinator (HUC) is an essential member of a healthcare team who ensures smooth communication between patients, nurses, doctors, and other medical staff. This role requires excellent organizational skills, attention to detail, and strong interpersonal abilities. If you're interested in pursuing this rewarding career path, here are some steps to help guide you: Step 1: Understand the Role and Responsibilities Before diving into becoming a HUC, familiarize yourself with its job description. Some common responsibilities include: * Answering phones and directing calls * Managing patient records * Scheduling appointments or procedures * Transcribing physician orders * Preparing and maintaining various reports related to patient care * Interacting with visitors, families, and hospital personnel Understanding these tasks will give you an idea if this position aligns with your strengths and interests. Key Tip: Being comfortable using computers and electronic health record systems is crucial as most hospitals have digitized their documentation processes. Step 2: Obtain Necessary Education and Training Although formal education isn't always required to become a HUC, many employers prefer candidates with high school diplomas or equivalents. Additionally, completing relevant certificate programs can enhance your employability. These typically cover topics such as medical terminology, keyboarding, insurance coding, billing practices, and pharmacology. Guideline: Check local community colleges, vocational schools, or online platforms offering accredited HUC courses. Step 3: Gain Experience through Internships or Volunteer Work Practical experience is valuable when applying for HUC positions. Consider internships at hospitals, clinics, or doctor's offices where you can learn directly from experienced professionals. Alternatively, volunteer work can also provide exposure to the healthcare environment and build soft skills necessary for the role. Key Tip: Highlight any customer service experiences in your resume since effective communication is vital in this profession. Step 4: Get Certified While not mandatory, obtaining certification shows commitment and expertise. The National Association of Health Unit Coordinators offers two levels of certifications - Certified Health Unit Coordinator (CHUC) and Master CHUC (MCHUC). Both require passing examinations that test knowledge in areas like medical ethics, legal issues, and administrative duties. Step 5: Apply for Jobs Begin searching for vacancies once you feel prepared. Websites like Indeed, LinkedIn, and Glassdoor often list available HUC positions. Tailor your resume to highlight relevant skills and qualifications. During interviews, emphasize your ability to multitask, stay organized, and maintain confidentiality. Guideline: Networking within the industry could lead to potential opportunities too. Attend job fairs, join professional organizations, and connect with current HUCs. Step 6: Continue Learning and Developing Professionally Once employed, continue enhancing your skills through workshops, seminars, and additional training offered by your employer or professional associations. Stay updated with changes in technology, regulations, and best practices in patient care. Remember, being a HUC involves constant interaction with people during stressful situations, so patience, empathy, and resilience are crucial traits. With dedication and passion, you'll be well on your way to making a significant impact on patients' lives! You can ruin your lipstick doin' that. oh, the pencil sharpener's not there wait. Photography is a way of making a picture using a camera. A person who makes using a camera is called a . A made using a camera is called a or photo. A "camera" is, in its form, like a box with a hole in the front. There is a special piece of glass in front of the hole called a lens. To take a photograph of , the lens makes a small picture of the object inside the camera. The lens does this by light. A lens in a camera works like a lens in glasses () or a glass. To make a photograph with a camera the button is . the button opens the shutter. The shutter is like a door. It covers the hole in the camera box. The shutter is behind the lens. When the shutter is closed no light can enter the camera box. When the shutter is open light can enter the camera. When the button is pressed the shutter opens and then closes. This very fast. The amount of time the shutter stays open for is called the shutter speed. The shutter speed can change between 1/1000th of a second (0.001 s) to a few seconds. the time taken for the shutter to open and close is much less than 1 second. Knowing why dogs bite and what to do to keep yourself safe is important. Dogs may bite for the following reasons: The noises and movements you make when you play are very exciting to dogs. When dogs play with other dogs, they often play roughly with their sharp teeth and claws. Sometimes dogs forget that they can't play the same way with you. Because dogs don't have hands, they use their mouths to grab things. A dog can hurt you by accident, just by being too excited. What you should do: Play gently and calmly. If a dog gets too excited, freeze until he calms down, then walk away. Take some time out before returning to play to give you both a chance to calm down. When a dog is in pain, he doesn't understand where the pain comes from. If you touch him, he may think you are causing the pain and could bite you to stop the pain. What you should do: If a dog is acting like he is sick or hurt, leave him alone - even if he belongs to your family. Tell an adult and together you can get medical help for the dog. A dog may protect anything that's important to him: his toys, his bed, his food and water bowls, his people, his yard, his house or even his car. If you come near something that a dog feels is off limits to you, he may bite to make you leave his property alone! What you should (or should not) do: Never go into a yard where there's a dog you don't know. Don't reach through a car window or a fence to pet a dog. Don't pet a dog that's tied up. Don't touch a dog's property, including his bed and food dishes. Quick movements and sudden or loud noises are scary for dogs, and they may bite to protect themselves. If a dog thinks you might hurt him, he may not know how to get away, so he'll protect himself by biting. What you should do: When you're around a dog you don't know, be quiet and move slowly. Always ask the dog's owner for permission before you pet him. If the owner says you may pet the dog, hold out your hand with your palm down. Let the dog come to you to sniff the back of your hand. Always pet him under the chin first. If a dog is sleeping, leave him alone. You can come back to pet or play with him after he wakes up. If the owner isn't there for you to ask permission to pet the dog, then LEAVE THE DOG ALONE! Watch and listen for the warnings that a dog will give you to let you know when he is upset. If his ears are laid back against his head or his legs are very stiff, he is probably warning you that he feels threatened and will protect himself if he must. If the hair on his back is standing up, that's another warning. If a dog is growling or barking with his teeth showing, it means he is ready to bite. A dog's warning signs mean that you're doing something he doesn't like, so stop doing it! If you think a dog is about to bite you: What you should NOT do: If you are bitten by a dog, or any animal, you should: Copyright Dumb Friends League. All rights reserved. Creating a slideshow can be an effective way to showcase your photos, present information, or tell a story. With modern technology, creating a professional-looking slideshow is easier than ever before. This tutorial will guide you through the process using Microsoft PowerPoint, but many other programs offer similar features. **Step 1: Gather Your Content** Before starting the slideshow, collect all the content that you want to include such as text, pictures, videos, charts, etc. Organize them into separate folders according to their type for easy access during creation. *Key tip*: Make sure the resolution of your images is high enough (at least 72 dpi) so they don't appear pixelated when presented in full screen. **Step 2: Open PowerPoint and Select a Template** Launch Microsoft PowerPoint on your computer. You'll see several options including 'Blank Presentation,' 'Recent,' 'Templates,' etc., click on 'Templates.' Here, you can choose from various predesigned templates based on themes like business, education, personal, holiday, etc. Choose one that suits your needs best. *Guideline*: If none of the provided templates meet your requirements, feel free to create a custom design by choosing 'Blank Presentation'. **Step 3: Create New Slides** Once you have selected a template, it's time to add new slides. To do this, look at the bottom left corner where you'll find 'New Slide'. Clicking on it will give you different layouts to select from depending upon what kind of slide you need - title only, bulleted list, picture with caption, etc. *Key Tip*: Keep the number of slides reasonable; too few might leave out important points while too many could bore the audience. A good rule of thumb is one slide per minute. **Step 4: Add Titles and Text** Each slide should have a clear title indicating its main topic. Double-click inside the box labeled 'Click to add Title', then enter your text. Title: How to Build a Slingshot Out of a Paperclip Introduction: Making a slingshot from a paperclip is an easy and fun DIY project that you can do with just a few household items. This miniature slingshot can be used as a stress reliever or for launching small projectiles. However, always remember to handle it responsibly and safely. Never aim or fire at people, animals, or breakable objects. Things You'll Need: 1. A medium-sized paper clip 2. A thin rubber band (about 8 inches long) 3. Tape (preferably electrical tape) 4. Scissors 5. Small piece of fabric or cloth (optional) Step 1: Straighten the Paper Clip Unbend the paper clip so that it forms a straight line. Use pliers if necessary for better control. Make sure the ends are pointy but safe enough to handle without causing injury. Key Tip: Be careful while straightening the paper clip to avoid any sharp edges which might cut your fingers. Step 2: Create the Pouch Cut a tiny square shape out of the fabric or cloth. It should be big enough to hold your ammunition securely yet small enough to fit within the span of the bent paper clip. If you don't have any fabric available, you could also use a strip of paper instead. Guideline: The size of the pouch depends on what kind of object you plan to shoot. For small pebbles or paper wads, a half-inch by half-inch pouch will suffice. Step 3: Attach the Pouch Loop one end of the rubber band around the middle part of the straightened paper clip. Secure it tightly using tape, ensuring there's no room for movement. Then attach the other end of the rubber band onto the bottom corner of the pouch. Again, secure it properly with tape. Key Tip: When attaching the rubber bands, ensure they aren't too tight or too loose. They need to provide enough tension to propel the object but shouldn't snap back harshly. Step 4: Form the Frame Bend both ends of the paper clip upwards into a Y shape. Each arm should measure about an inch in length. These arms serve as handles for holding the slingshot steady when pulling back the rubber band. Step 5: Test Your Slingshot Before loading anything into the pouch, test your slingshot by pulling back the rubber band and releasing it several times. Check if the mechanism works smoothly and doesn't put excessive strain on the paper clip. Safety Guideline: Always keep your face away from the direction where the slingshot is aimed even during testing phase. Step 6: Load and Fire! Now you're ready to load your ammo. Place your chosen item gently into the pouch, pull back the rubber band, take aim, and release! Remember, this simple slingshot has limitations due to its size and materials used. But building it provides a great introduction to understanding basic principles of physics and engineering. Happy shooting! Title: How to Avoid Temptation at the Grocery Store Shopping for groceries is an essential task that many people do weekly or even daily. However, grocery stores are designed to entice customers into making impulsive purchases, which can lead to overspending and unhealthy food choices. To help you make better decisions while shopping for groceries, we have created this comprehensive guide with 15 steps to avoid temptation at the grocery store. ### Step 1: Make a List Before going to the grocery store, create a list of items you need based on your meal plan for the week. This will keep you focused on what you actually require instead of wandering around aimlessly and being swayed by attractive product displays. Having a clear list also saves time as you don't have to spend extra minutes deciding what to buy. Key Tip: Organize your list according to the layout of the store to minimize backtracking. Desired Outcome: A well-planned list ensures that you stick to buying only necessary items, reducing chances of impulse buys. ### Step 2: Eat Before Shopping Never go grocery shopping when you're hungry. Research shows that shoppers who visit the supermarket on an empty stomach tend to purchase more high-calorie foods than those who aren't hungry. Key Guideline: Have a nutritious snack or meal before heading out to the store. Desired Outcome: By satisfying your hunger prior to shopping, you reduce the likelihood of succumbing to tempting but unnecessary food options. ### Step 3: Choose the Right Time Try to schedule your grocery trips during off-peak hours. Not only does this mean less crowded aisles, but it may also decrease stress levels and allow for mindful decision-making. Key Tip: Early morning or late evening slots often have fewer crowds compared to after-work rush hours. Desired Outcome: Less crowd leads to less distraction, enabling you to focus more on sticking to your list. ### Step 4: Stick to Your List The other stable-boys had heard him doing this, so they peeped through the key-hole into his room, and saw that he was kneeling there before a picture; so they told everyone that every morning and evening the youth knelt down and prayed to an idol which he had; and at last they went to the King himself, and begged that he too would peep through the key-hole, and see for himself what the youth did. At first the King would not believe this, but after a long, long time, they prevailed with him, and he crept on tip-toe to the door, peeped through, and saw the youth on his knees, with his hands clasped together before a picture which was hanging on the wall. Seven is called a square root of 49. So if a number is multiplied by itself to get the product, the number is the square root of the product. So that's the thing that we need to remember when we're trying to find roots. So let's try and do a few problems. We need to find in Part A the square root of 49. Now this is the symbol for square root or root. If there is no number around it; that means it's the square root. So if we are trying to find the square root for 49, we need to find a number which when multiplied by itself will give us 49. Well, what's that number, 7x7 gives me 49. So the square root of 49 is seven. All we need to do is find the number which when multiplied by itself will give you the product. The second thing we need to remember is a square root of 49 equals seven, then 7x7=49. So, square root of 49 equals seven, 7x7=49. They are reversible operations. So, let's do Part B. Part B is we need to find the cube root of -125. The number three before the root sign signifies that instead of the number being multiplied twice, it needs to be multiplied thrice. So which number when multiplied by itself three times will give us -125? Do we know what it is? It's actually -5, -5x-5 is +25, x -5 is -125. So -5 multiplied by itself three times gives us -125. So the cube root of -125=-5. That's the answer we're looking for. Let's do the third one right here, which is we need the square root of 1/4, which means what fraction when multiplied by itself gives us 1/4? Well, I just gave it to you. 1/2x1/2 gives us 1/4. Well, the number is 1/2. Let's try a slightly difficult problem which says evaluate the expression when A=9 and B=7. We need to take the square root of A+B. Well what is A+B? Let's substitute it. The binary numeral system is a way to write numbers using only two digits: 0 and 1. These are used in computers as a series of "off" and "on" switches. In binary, each digit's place value is twice as much as that of the next digit to the right (since each digit holds two values). In decimal - the system that humans normally use - each digit holds ten values, and the place value increases by a power of ten (ones, tens, hundreds, etc.). The place value of the rightmost digit, in either case, is 1. Once upon a time, there was a lonely monkey living in a jungle. Title: Change the Computer's Time and Date Using the Command Prompt Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through changing your computer's time and date using the Command Prompt. This method is particularly useful when other methods are unavailable or if you prefer working with command-line interfaces. It can be helpful in troubleshooting software that relies on accurate timestamps, setting up virtual machines, or simply checking your system's clock settings from a different perspective. Before starting this process, ensure you have administrative privileges on your computer. The steps outlined below may vary slightly depending on your operating system version; however, they should provide a solid foundation for making these changes across most Windows systems. Step 1: Open the Command Prompt as an Administrator Press `Win + X` keys simultaneously (or right-click the Start button) to open the Power User menu. From there, select 'Command Prompt (Admin)' or 'Windows Terminal (Admin)' to launch the console with elevated permissions. Confirm any prompts requesting permission to continue. Opening the Command Prompt in administrator mode ensures you have sufficient access rights to modify critical system components like time and date settings. Step 2: Check Current System Date and Time Type `w32tm /date` at the prompt and press Enter. You'll see something similar to this output: ``` The story of Saint George and the Dragon symbolizes good winning over evil. Compare Book of Revelation chapters 12-13. When a flock of birds is feeding it is common for one bird to perch on a high place to keep guard over the flock. In the same way, when a flock is asleep, often, one bird will remain awake. It is also common for large flocks to send one or two birds ahead of them when they are flying to a new area. The look-out birds can spy the lie of the land to find food, water and good places to perch. Sharks have the ability to determine the direction of a given scent based on the timing of scent detection in each nostril. This is similar to the method mammals use to determine direction of sound. Sam hopped over to them and he said, "What is wrong here? Why are you both so angry?" The rabbit said, "That squirrel won't share his nuts with me." The squirrel said, "But you didn't ask nicely!" First, they anchor the plant to the ground. Second, they absorb water and various nutrients dissolved in water from the soil. Plants use the water to make food. How toddlers use pronouns: At around age two, your child will realize there are quicker ways to say "the boy" and "the girl," but she may call boys "she" and "her" and girls "he" or "him" or refer to everyone as one gender. Your teensy talker may even call people "it," even though she's aware that people are very different from inanimate objects. Why toddlers have a tough time using pronouns: Your tot's still working on her language development, so she hasn't heard enough people use these words and needs more time to practice them, say experts at the American Academy of Pediatrics. What you need to know about pronoun problems: Toddlers start becoming pros at using pronouns at around age two and a half, but if your child doesn't have pronouns down pat by then, mention it at your next pediatrician visit. Your doctor might suggest that you practice using pronouns together for a few months and then report back on your progress. Still no dice at that point? You may be referred to a speech pathologist, especially if she's still having trouble with other toddler words. How to help your toddler use pronouns: - Narrate your day. Toddler learning is always in the air - you'd be surprised at how much she takes in from what's going on around her. So tell your child what's going on by using pronouns. Say something like, "The mail carrier came! I'm going to see what she left for us. Then I'll start cooking dinner. Do you think Daddy will like what I make for him?" Hearing everyday language will help your little one learn how to use pronouns. - Model the right way to use pronouns. Although you may be embarrassed when your pint-sized princess calls another little girl at the playground "he," avoid saying, "That's a girl - not a boy." For one, she probably already knows that the potential playmate she's spied is a girl. For another, she's more likely to learn from hearing the right pronoun to use. So say, "I see that little girl over there, too. She looks nice. Let's go talk to her." - Play games. Try a toddler speech learning activity, like a game of "I Spy." When you're out and about, point out items people are wearing or holding. You could say, "I spy something red." When your child finds the correct object, say, "Yes, that boy is wearing red. He is wearing red sneakers." No d' you know what this spells? Supposing a journey to the other side of the Milky Way, the total time for a message and its reply would be about 200,000 years. Even more seriously, no spacecraft could travel faster than light, so all galactic-scale transport would be effectively one-way, and would take much longer than than any modern civilisation has existed. [SIS may be blocking Chi's access to the salt] Title: How to Configure Wi-Fi on Your iPod Touch Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through configuring Wi-Fi on your iPod Touch. By following these steps, you'll be able to connect your device to wireless networks, enabling internet access for various applications such as web browsing, email, music streaming, and social media. Let's get started! Step 1: Access Settings App First, locate and open the 'Settings' app on your iPod Touch home screen. This preinstalled application allows users to customize settings related to their device's functionality, including network connections like Wi-Fi. Step 2: Enable Wi-Fi Feature Within the Settings menu, select the option labeled 'Wi-Fi'. Toggle the switch next to 'Wi-Fi' towards the right to turn it ON (the switch should become green). Enabling this feature ensures that your iPod Touch searches for available wireless networks within range. Key Tip: If you cannot see the Wi-Fi option or if it is grayed out, make sure Airplane Mode isn't activated. Go to Control Center by swiping down from the upper right corner of the screen and ensure the airplane icon is off. Step 3: Search for Available Networks After turning on the Wi-Fi feature, your iPod Touch automatically scans for nearby wireless networks. Wait until the list populates under the 'Choose A Network...' heading. The name(s) of visible network(s) will appear here. Guideline: Keep in mind that some networks may require additional actions before they can be accessed (e.g., accepting terms & conditions, providing login credentials), while others might remain hidden due to security reasons. Step 4: Select Desired Network Tap the name of the network you wish to join. Once selected, your iPod Touch attempts to establish a connection with the chosen network. Step 5: Enter Password (If Necessary) For secured networks, an input field will prompt you to enter the correct password. Type in the required characters carefully, ensuring capitalization and special symbols are accurate. After entering the password correctly, hit 'Join' at the top right corner. Key Tips: - Forgetting a saved network's password? Try resetting the router, checking the documentation provided by your Internet Service Provider (ISP), or asking someone who has access to the network for assistance. - Can't remember which password you used previously? Check whether your Mac or PC has connected to the same network; they might store the password history. Step 6: Verify Connection Status Once successfully connected, the network's name will display beneath 'Choose A Network...', preceded by 'Connected to:' text. Additionally, a blue tick mark appears beside the connected network's name. At this point, your iPod Touch is ready to utilize the internet via the established Wi-Fi connection. Step 7: Adjust Additional Wi-Fi Options (Optional) By tapping the 'Info' button (blue 'i' inside a circle) next to the connected network, several advanced options become accessible. These include Auto-Join, HTTP PROXY setup, IPv6 configuration, DNS settings, etc. Unless necessary, avoid changing these unless you have prior networking knowledge. Conclusion: Congratulations! You've now learned how to configure Wi-Fi on your iPod Touch. Enjoy seamless internet connectivity across various apps and services offered by Apple and third parties alike. Remember always to secure your Wi-Fi networks properly to protect your personal data and maintain optimal performance levels. Figure Out Your Fictionkin Type ============================= Fictionkin is a term used within the otherkin community to describe individuals who identify as being non-human fictional characters or creatures from books, movies, TV shows, video games, mythology, or folklore. This guide will help you explore your feelings and determine if fictionkin identity resonates with you. Remember that self-discovery can be a complex and ongoing process; this article aims to provide guidance and support without making assumptions about your personal experiences or beliefs. ### Understanding Kin Types Before diving into fictionkin identification, let's clarify some common terms: 1. **Kin type**: The category of beings someone identifies as (e.g., dragon, elf, fictional character). 2. **Otherkin**: Individuals identifying as any kind of non-human entity, including but not limited to animals, mythological creatures, aliens, and fictionkin. 3. **Therianthropy**: A subcategory of otherkin referring specifically to those who feel they are part animal. 4. **Plurality**: An umbrella term encompassing all forms of multiple consciousnesses residing within one physical body, such as systems, multiples, DID (Dissociative Identity Disorder), walk-ins, etc. 5. **Fictionkin**: People who identify as fictional characters or creatures from various media sources. **Key Tip:** Keep an open mind throughout this journey, acknowledging that there isn't only one way to define yourself or experience kinship. It's perfectly okay if you don't fit neatly into these categories or prefer different labels altogether. ### Step 1: Reflect On Your Connection To Fictional Characters Or Creatures Noah (meaning rest or comfort) is a man in the Abrahamic religions. He was the son of Lamech, and the father of sons, Shem, Ham, Canaan, and Japheth. Noah and his ark is in the Book of Genesis, which also describes Noah as the 'first man to plant a vineyard' (chapter 9). Several other parts of the Bible mention him, as does the Quran. Now for the SCIENCE part.... This POLYMER is unique because it has qualities of both a solid and a liquid. It can take the shape of its containers like a liquid does, yet you can hold it in your hand and pick it up like a solid. As you might know, solid molecules are tight together, liquid molecules spread out and break apart (drops) POLYMER molecules CHAIN themselves together (they can stretch and bend like chains) and that makes them special. Jell-O, rubber bands, plastic soda bottles, sneaker soles, even gum are all forms of polymers. The polymer you made should be kept in a sealed plastic bag when you aren't playing with it. Also, be sure to keep it away from young kids or pets who might think it's food. Have fun! Recap: What is a polymer? Both a solid and a liquid. Molecules which are tiny little molecules chain together which makes an item hold its shape and then can be altered. What are some other examples of polymers? From a practical point of view, this means that Newton's laws of motion are valid in all inertial systems, which means those at rest or those moving with constant speed relative to one considered at rest. This is the law of inertia: a body at rest continues at rest and a body in motion continues in motion in a straight line unless influenced by an external force. A Galilean coordinate system is one where the law of inertia is valid. The laws of mechanics of Galileo and Newton are valid in a Galilean coordinate system. If K is a Galilean coordinate system, then every other system K' is a Galilean coordinate system if it lies at rest or moves according to the law of inertia relative to K. Relative to K', the mechanical laws of Galileo and Newton are as valid as they are relative to K. [8 sec; tape OFF/ON at 225; 7 sec silence, clunks] XXX leave the ketchup thing on I don't know whatever. students to be fluent readers, they must read quickly, automatically, and expressively. Not only should students read with speed, ease, and expression, should also comprehend what they are reading. One way to increase fluency is through repeated readings. Reading a passage of text several times allows students to become more fluent readers. In this lesson, students will gain fluency through repeated one minute reads. piece of cardstock with the sentence "The frog hopped off the lily pad to catch a fly." for every group, every group, one copy of Days with Frog and Toad for every group, Cardstock with several lily pads on it students to move their frog for each student, cut out frog die cut for student, one copy of Jane and Babe. - Explain to students what being a fluent reader means. "It is very important that we all become fluent readers. To be a fluent reader, you must read with speed and ease. A fluent reader also comprehends what they are reading. When we are fluent readers our reading sounds much nicer and smoother. It also allows us to enjoy what we are reading!" - Demonstrate a fluent reader and a non-fluent reader. "To review what it means to be a fluent reader and non-fluent reader I am going to read a page from the book Jane and Babe. Bbbbbbaabe s-s-tays iiiin hi-i-s caaaggge. (Read one sentence from the book) (The first time read the sentence very slowly, without expression, and choppy.) That is what a beginning reader sounds like. They read very slow, choppy, and without expression. Now I am going to read the same page from Jane and Babe. Babe stays in his cage. This time I am going to read like a fluent reader. (This time read the text faster, with ease, and expression.) That it what a fluent reader sounds like. They read the text quickly, smoothly, and with expression. Our goal is for everyone to be a fluent reader." - Explain to students that they are going to be doing repeated readings. "To make you a more fluent reader, we are going to do a repeated readings. I am going to put you with a partner and you are going to take turns reading. I am going to give each group a sentence on a piece of paper. I want each of you to read the sentence aloud to your partner. The first time you may be a little slow and choppy, but that's okay because you are going to read the sentence four more times to your partner. By the end, you will be reading more fluently because you are familiar with the words and the sentence will make more sense." Pass out to each group a piece of cardstock with the sentence "The frog hopped off the lily pad to catch a fly". - Explain to the students that they are going to be doing one minute reads. "Now we are going to do a one minute read. Each group is going to be reading the book Days with Frog and Toad. Frog and Toad are very good friends. They like to do fun things together. You'll have to read the book to find out what fun things Frog and Toad do. (Pass out each group a copy of Days with Frog and Toad and a stopwatch) While one person is reading their partner will be timing them. The reader should read as many words as they can. If you come to a tricky word, remember to use your cover-up. Try your best! The reader will read for one minute four times. The timer will time one minute. After each one minute, the timer will write down the number of words the reader read. Everyone has a piece of paper with several lily pads drawn on it that lead to a fly and a frog! Each time you want your reading to get faster so the frog can catch the fly! The lily pads all have different numbers on them. After each one minute read the timer will move the frog to the lily pad with the number of words the reader read. With each reading, you should get closer and closer to the fly! After the first person reads four times switch. I will be walking around the room. If you need help, just raise your hand. Assessment: After everyone has completed their one minute reads, I will walk around the room and everyone's frog chart to see how the student's fluency progressed. I will then take each student individually and do two one minute reads on the book Days with Frog and Toad. Calculate the first six terms of this sequence. What do you notice? Calculate a few more terms and find the squares of the terms. Can you prove that the special property you notice about this sequence will apply to all the later terms of the sequence? Write down a formula to give an approximation to the cube root of a number and test it for the cube root of 3 and the cube root of 8. How many terms of the sequence do you have to take before you get the cube root of 8 correct to as many decimal places as your calculator will give? Scheduling games is a little more challenging than one might desire. Here are some tournament formats that sport schedulers use. Take some numbered cards; between ten and twenty should be enough (you could use a suit from a pack of playing cards). Shuffle the cards. Then organise your deck of cards into numerical order. What method did you use to put them in numerical order? Can you think of any other ways you could have sorted them? Here are some different sorting algorithms you could try. You may find it easiest to lay the cards out in a line to keep track of their order and see what's happening at a glance. Try each algorithm a few times, and keep a record of how many 'moves' or 'swaps' you do. You could work with a friend and 'race' against each other to see who sorts their pack the quickest. If you are struggling to make sense of the written algorithms, here are some videos showing each algorithm being performed. Listen and gather facts The first step is often the hardest for parents: listen to your child's whole story without interrupting. Your goal is to try to figure out what happened, who was involved, where and when the teasing took place, and why your child was teased. Unfortunately, teasing is a part of growing up, but some kids seem to get more than their fair share of insults. If your child appears to be in no immediate danger, keep listening to find out how she reacts to the bullying. By knowing what reaction didn't stop the bully, you can offer your child a more effective option. Here are a few: - Assert yourself. Teach child to face the bully by standing tall and using a strong voice. Your child should name the bullying behavior and tell the aggressor to stop: "That's teasing. Stop it." or "Stop making fun of me. It's mean." - Question the response. Ann Bishop, who teaches violence prevention curriculums, tells her students to respond to an insult with a nondefensive question: "Why would you say that?" or "Why would you want to tell me I am dumb (or fat) and hurt my feelings?" - Use "I want." Communication experts suggest teaching your child to address the bully beginning with "I want" and say firmly what he wants changed: "I want you to leave me alone." or "I want you to stop teasing me." - Agree with the teaser. Consider helping your child create a statement agreeing with her teaser. Teaser; "You're dumb." Child: "Yeah, but I'm good at it." or Teaser: "Hey, four eyes." Child: "You're right, my eyesight is poor." - Ignore it. Bullies love it when their teasing upsets their victims, so help your child find a way to not let his tormentor get to him. A group of fifth graders told me ways they ignore their teasers: "Pretend they're invisible," "Walk away without looking at them," "Quickly look at something else and laugh," and "Look completely uninterested. 2. Teach a bully-proofing strategy What may work with one child may not with another, so it's best to discuss a range of options and then choose the one or two your child feels most comfortable with. - Make Fun of the Teasing. Fred Frankel, author of Good Friends Are Hard to Find suggests victims answer every tease with a reply, but not tease back. The teasing often stops, Frankel says, because the child lets the tormentor know he's not going to let the teasing get to him (even if it does). Suppose the teaser says, "You're stupid." The child says a rehearsed comeback such as: "Really?" Other comebacks could be: "So?," "You don't say," "And your point is?," or "Thanks for telling me." 3. Rehearse the strategy with your child Once you choose a technique, rehearse it together so your child is comfortable trying it. The trick is for your child to deliver it assuredly to the bully-and that takes practice. Explain that though he has the right to feel angry, it's not okay to let it get out of control. Besides, anger just fuels the bully. Try teaching your child the CALM approach to defueling the tormentor. - C - Cool down. When you confront the bully, stay calm and always in control. Don't let him think he's getting to you. If you need to calm down, count to twenty slowly inside your head or say to yourself, "Chill out!" And most importantly: tell your child to always get help whenever there is a chance she might be injured. - A - Assert yourself. Try the strategy with the bully just like you practiced. - L - Look at the teaser straight in the eye. Appear confident, hold your head high and stand tall. - M - Mean it! Use a firm, strong voice. Say what you feel, but don't be insulting, threaten or tease back. Throw 'em in the brig on bread an'... Put the pictures in Mommy's pocket, can you? You're going to get dirty on the floor. Bringing an off-screen window back onto the visible screen is a common task when using windows operating system. This tutorial will guide you through various methods to bring an off-screen window back on Windows. Method 1: Using Keyboard Shortcuts ---------------------------------- ### Step 1: Activate the Application The first step is to activate the application that contains the off-screen window. You can do this by clicking on its icon in the taskbar or by pressing Alt + Tab until the correct application is highlighted. With his friend Tim Staffell, Brian May started a band called 1984 in the mid 1960's. The band broke up after a little bit and then May decided to make another band and teamed up with two other college students named Tim Staffell and Roger Taylor. They called themselves "Smile" with Roger Taylor on drums and vocals, Tim Staffell on vocals and bass and Brian May on guitar and vocals. They did a few hits such as "April Lady". Tim Staffell became friends with another college student, Farrokh Bulsara (to be later known as Freddie Mercury) and Farrokh became a big fan of "Smile" and encouraged them a lot. Later on, Tim left "Smile" to join "Humpy Bongs" and Farrokh Bulsara subsequently joined the band on vocals and piano in 1970. Then Farrokh came up with the name Queen, so they changed it from "Smile" to "Queen". They then started auditions for a new bassist. They were going through several bassists during this time and none of them stuck, only lasting for about a few minutes. None of the auditioners managed to suit the place of the new bassist, but finally in 1971, they settled on John Deacon. Queen's first album was released in 1973 and it was called "Queen" with songs such as "Seven Seas Of Rhye" and "Liar". Usually showering is segregated by sex. We are all familiar with plants and the roots that anchor them into the ground and provide them with nutrients and moisture. What you may not have thought about is how they can show stratigraphic up if the roots are preserved in the rock record. The photo on the left is an example of a modern plant and how it grows. You can see the main trunk of the tree and - because the river has eroded the soil from underneath - the branching nature of the root below the surface. Typical of all plants, the part of the plant that is at the top of the bed is a single, large column. As you go deeper into the earth, the root mass branches out and gets thinner, eventually coming to very fine, tapered ends. Typically, the top part of a plant is not preserved. The roots, however, sometimes do get saved. The photograph on the right shows just such a feature. There is a thin orange-colored horizon under which very fine roots can be seen. The orange layer is known as a paleosol, or old soil, and marks a surface that once saw the light of day and was exposed as topsoil. The roots that are found below are all that remains of the plants that once populated this area. You can tell that this photo is in the stratigraphic up position in two ways. First, the roots start as single, larger structures towards the top and branch out and get thinner towards the bottom. Second, the paleosol layer overlying the roots also shows that this is stratigraphically up in orientation. These two photos show root structures that are 300 million years old. The one on the left is the original photograph, and the one on the right has had red lines added to outline some of the roots to make them easier to find. (Click the photo to enlarge it and see the red lines.) These photos also show rocks in the stratigraphic up position. en Espanol Spanish version - roots (Microsoft Word 33kB Feb2 09) What else can you put in there? Once upon a time, there lived a green snake. He wanted some friends so he spread out to look for them. He spread through the grass, went into some bushes and even snuck under a pile of rocks. But the green snake couldn't find any friends. Then he spread out a bit more, and then he saw something awesome! Two other snakes! They were a lot like him except one was red and one was blue. The green snake rushed over and said hello. The other two snakes were really happy to meet him. The three snakes began to play together, having all kinds of fun. All day long they spread around and chased each other, and in the evening they snuggled up together and slept. The green snake had lots of fun with his new friends. They played together every day and the green snake was very happy to have them. The three friends will always be together and spread their joy! Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. An important Catholic prayer is the Rosary. This prayer is done with Rosary beads used to count each prayer. The Rosary is made up of five decades, each decade being made up of 1 Our Father, 10 Hail Marys, and 1 Glory Be, as well as saying the Sacred Mysteries. It is made up of passages taken from scripture. It is recommended by the clergy to continue the very old practice of praying the Rosary daily, as it has not only many powerful requests but is made up of words taken directly from scripture, like the Lord's Prayer (Our Father) and the Hail Mary. The Rosary is not complete unless one thinks about the passion of Christ while praying out loud. In addition, the clergy (bishops, priests, and deacons) must pray the Divine Office. yeah, don't try to push it down you might break it. NARRATOR: One of the most important discoveries of the Vikings, was the invention of the "keel." A keel is a long narrow piece of wood, attached to the underside of a ship. The keel reduced the ship's rolling motion. By doing so, it greatly improved the ship's speed and thus the distance it could travel with out stopping for supplies. A ship with a keel is less likely to be blown sideways by the wind. This make the ship easier to steer, and enabled the Vikings to stay on course and reach their destination. As we sail through life, we sometimes need a "keel" to help keep us on course. Principle we learn as Scouts can be our keel, if we strive to live by the Cub Scout Promise and the Law of the Pack. Once upon a time, in a small garden, there lived a lazy insect named Izzy. Izzy did not like to work hard like the other insects. She loved to sit in the sun and watch the flowers grow. One day, Izzy found a big, red apple. She was so happy and wanted to eat it all by herself. But, she was too lazy to carry the apple to her home. So, she left it under the sun to eat it later. The next day, Izzy came back to eat her apple. But, the hot sun had spoiled the apple. It was not good to eat anymore. Izzy was sad and hungry. She learned that being lazy can spoil good things. From that day on, Izzy worked hard with the other insects and always shared her food. One day there was a good dog. His name was Steve. Steve always wanted to learn more and do good things, so he went on a journey. Steve asked people, "Do you know any news that can help me?" Most of the people he asked ignored him, but then one day he met a nice lady. She said, "I know some news that will help you be even better!" The lady smiled as she said to Steve, "I heard that every day if you do good things then you will become an even better person!" So Steve thanked her and said, "Thank you for not ignoring me!" The bacteria that are on teeth eat into the enamel. Cleaning and flossing teeth, eating good foods, and having a dentist take off plaque make less bacteria on teeth. If there is too much bacteria, they eat enamel faster than teeth make enamel. This makes holes in enamel called cavities. When a person gets cavities, he has the disease dental caries. Making cavities in enamel happens slowly. But once cavities go through enamel, the soft dentine is hurt much faster. Cavities may be fixed by dentists. Today, let's learn about histograms and how they can help us understand data. A histogram is a type of chart that displays data using bars. It helps us visualize the distribution of data by showing how often certain values appear in our dataset. Let me give you an example to make it clearer. Imagine that your teacher asked you to find out how many books each student in your class has read this month. You collect the data and notice that some students have read between 0-5 books, others between 5-10 books, and so on. To represent this data visually, you could create a histogram like the one below: [Insert image of a histogram here] In this histogram, each bar represents a range or "class interval." The height of the bar tells us how many students fall into that category. So, looking at the first bar, we can see that 4 students have read between 0-5 books this month. Similarly, we can see that 7 students have read between 5-10 books. Now, let's try to answer some questions based on the histogram. Q: How wide are the class intervals? A: Each class interval is 5 units wide because the difference between the upper and lower limits of each class is always 5. For example, the first class interval goes from 0 to 5, while the second class interval ranges from 5 to 10. Q: Which age group reads the least number of books? A: Based on the histogram, students who have read between 0-5 books form the smallest group. This means that these students read the least number of books this month. Q: Which age group reads the most number of books? A: Looking at the histogram again, we can see that students who have read between 5-10 books form the largest group. Therefore, these students read the most number of books this month. I hope this explanation helped you better understand histograms and their uses! Remember, practice makes perfect, so keep creating histograms with different types of datasets to get comfortable with them. Happy graphing! Title: How to Choose a Dog Leash Introduction: A dog leash is an essential tool for every dog owner. It ensures your pet's safety during walks, helps with training, and allows you to maintain control in various environments. However, choosing the right leash can be overwhelming due to the wide array of options available. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of selecting the perfect leash for your furry friend. Step 1: Determine Your Needs Consider why you need a leash. Is it for basic walking, heavy-duty control, or training purposes? Understanding your needs will help narrow down your choices. For instance, retractable leashes are great for casual strolls but may not provide sufficient control for rigorous training sessions. Key Tip: Always prioritize safety and comfort over aesthetics when choosing a dog leash. Step 2: Select the Right Material Leashes come in different materials such as nylon, leather, chain, and rope. Nylon is durable, lightweight, and easy to clean; however, it might cause discomfort if it rubs against your dog's skin harshly. Leather looks stylish and feels soft yet strong, although it requires regular maintenance. Chain leashes offer maximum durability and visibility but could potentially harm your dog if they chew on it. Rope leashes are flexible, comfortable, and gentle on dogs' coats but tend to wear out faster than other materials. Guideline: Opt for a material that suits both your lifestyle and your dog's needs. Step 3: Measure Your Dog's Size The length and width of the leash should match your dog's size. A large breed dog would require a wider and stronger leash compared to smaller ones. Similarly, longer leashes are suitable for larger dogs who need more freedom while shorter leashes work well for small dogs or those prone to pulling. Key Tip: As a general rule, the leash should be about 4-6 feet long for most situations. Anything longer than this may make controlling your dog difficult. Step 4: Check the Hardware Inspect the clasp, handle, and any additional features like traffic handles or swivel connectors. They should be robust, rust-resistant, and secure enough to hold your dog without breaking or causing injury. Swivel connectors prevent tangling which is particularly useful for active dogs. Step 5: Consider Special Features Some leashes have special features designed for specific uses. These include hands-free leads for joggers, double-ended leads for multiple dogs, or even built-in flashlights for nighttime walks. Think about what extra functions would benefit you and your pet before making a decision. Step 6: Try It Out If possible, test the leash with your dog before purchasing. Make sure it feels comfortable in your hand, doesn't chafe against your dog's neck, and provides adequate control. Remember, the best leash is one that meets all your requirements and enhances your bonding experience with your pet. Conclusion: Selecting the ideal dog leash involves careful consideration of several factors including purpose, material, size, hardware quality, special features, and user experience. By following these steps, you'll find a leash that caters to both yours and your dog's preferences, ensuring safe and enjoyable adventures together. By: Reading Rockets (2007) Have Growing Readers delivered each month right to your inbox! (In English & Spanish) Sign up here > There's a reason we learn nursery rhymes as young children. They help us develop an ear for our language. Rhyme and rhythm highlight the sounds and syllables in words. And understanding sounds and syllables helps kids learn to read! Activities for younger children - Create word families. Use refrigerator magnets to spell a word ending (-ap). Have your child put other letters in front of the word ending to create rhyming words (tap, cap, map, lap). - Fill in the blank. Read children's poems aloud and leave off the final word, having your child fill in the missing rhyming word: "Run, run, as fast as you can, You can't catch me, I'm the gingerbread ____." - Make TV time a time for learning. Choose programs with a focus on reading. Check out PBS' Between the Lions, for children ages 4-7, or Reading Rainbow, for ages 4-8. Activities for older children - Write a swap poem. You write one line of a poem, and have your child write the next line, matching the rhythm, and rhyming the last word. (Hint: Use words that are easy to rhyme!) - Create a rhyming dictionary. Have a page for -op words, and -end words, and -ing words, and -oat words, and then move on to more complex letter patterns like -ouble (trouble, double). - If kids don't like reading, try poetry! Poems are short, provide important practice with reading fluency, and give reluctant readers a sense of accomplishment. Some good poetry books for kids: - Sing to the Sun, by Ashley Bryan - In the Eyes of a Cat: Japanese Poetry, by Demi - This Big Sky, by Pat Mora - The Random House Book of Poetry for Children, edited by Jack Prelutsky - A Child's Garden of Verses, by Robert Louis Stevenson - Any book by Dr. Seuss! *To view this file, you will need a copy of Acrobat Reader. If it is not already installed on your computer, click here to download. Greta nodded. Title: How to Be Beautiful at 13 Introduction: Puberty is a time of significant change in a young person's life, both physically and emotionally. It can be an exciting yet challenging period as you navigate through new experiences and self-discovery. This guide aims to provide practical advice on maintaining good personal hygiene, developing healthy habits, and enhancing your natural beauty during this transformative stage. Remember that true beauty comes from within, so focusing on confidence, kindness, and self-acceptance should always be your top priority. Step 1: Establish a Daily Skincare Routine * Importance: A consistent skincare routine can help maintain clear, healthy skin by removing impurities, controlling oil production, and preventing breakouts. * Tips: + Use a gentle cleanser suitable for your skin type (normal, oily, dry, or combination) twice daily. + Follow up with a toner to balance pH levels and prepare your skin for moisturizer. + Apply a lightweight moisturizer to keep skin hydrated and supple. + Consider using an eye cream to reduce puffiness and dark circles. + Don't forget sunscreen! Protecting your skin from harmful UV rays is essential for preventing premature aging and skin damage. Step 2: Develop Healthy Eating Habits * Importance: Consuming nutritious foods promotes overall health, which reflects in your appearance. Eating well also supports clear skin, strong hair, and improved mood. * Guidelines: + Focus on consuming whole fruits, vegetables, lean proteins, and whole grains. Title: How to Control Your Audio on Zoom Introduction: Zoom is a popular video conferencing platform that allows users to communicate with colleagues, friends, and family through audio and video calls. Controlling your audio settings on Zoom is crucial to ensure clear communication during meetings or events. This tutorial will guide you through various steps to control your audio on Zoom effectively. Step 1: Download and Install Zoom Before controlling your audio settings, download and install the latest version of Zoom from its official website (<https://zoom.us/download>). Once installed, launch the application and sign up for an account if necessary. Key Tips: Make sure you have administrative access to your device before installation. Guidelines: Follow the prompts to create a new account using your email address or log in to an existing one. Step 2: Joining a Meeting To join a meeting, click on the 'Join' button at the top right corner of the screen. Enter the meeting ID provided by the host and select whether you want to connect via computer audio or telephone. Key Tip: Test your speakers and microphone before joining a meeting to avoid any technical issues. Guideline: If prompted, allow Zoom permission to access your camera and microphone. Step 3: Accessing Audio Settings During a Call Once connected, locate the audio controls panel by clicking on the arrow next to the 'Mute' icon located near the bottom left-hand side of the window. A dropdown menu should appear displaying options related to speaker and microphone settings. Key Tip: Familiarize yourself with these settings so you can quickly adjust them when needed. Guideline: It's recommended to mute your microphone whenever you aren't speaking to reduce background noise. Step 4: Adjusting Speaker Settings Click on 'Speaker' within the audio controls panel. From here, choose the correct output device for hearing others during the call. You may also test your speakers by pressing the 'Test Speaker' button. The system will play a short tone indicating whether or not the selected device is working correctly. Key Tip: Always double-check this setting if you experience difficulty hearing other participants. Guideline: Consider wearing headphones instead of relying solely on built-in laptop speakers for better sound quality and privacy. Step 5: Configuring Microphone Settings Select 'Microphone' from the same audio controls panel used earlier. Choose the appropriate input device for your microphone. Similar to testing speakers, press the 'Test Mic' button to confirm functionality. Additionally, check the volume meter to see if your voice levels are adequate; aim for green bars without reaching red ones as they indicate potential distortion. Key Tip: Keep your microphone close to minimize ambient noises. Guideline: Use push-to-talk features if environmental sounds interfere significantly with your speech. Step 6: Advanced Audio Options For more advanced configurations, click on the gear icon found at the upper right corner of the audio controls panel. Here, you can enable stereo audio, echo cancellation, suppress persistent background noise, and adjust automatic gain control. Key Tip: Experiment with different combinations based on individual preferences and requirements. Guideline: Remember that excessive tinkering might lead to suboptimal results; only modify what's essential. Conclusion: Controlling your audio on Zoom ensures smooth and uninterrupted communication during virtual gatherings. By following the above steps meticulously, you'll be able to manage your audio settings proficiently, making every interaction productive and enjoyable. Study the following sentences. - They started in the morning. (Started when? - in the morning) - They started before the sun rose. (started when? - before the sun rose) It is clear that in sentence 1 and sentence 2 the groups of words in italics do the work of an adverb as they modify the verb started, showing when the action was performed. Here the group of words 'in the morning' is an adverb phrase. It does not have a subject or a predicate of its own. It makes sense, but not complete sense. Now consider the group of words 'before the sun rose'. It has a subject and a predicate of its own. Still, it is not a complete sentence. In fact, it is part of a large sentence. Such a group of words which forms part of a sentence, and has a subject and a predicate of its own is called a clause. Since the clause 'before the sun rose' does the work of an adverb it is called an adverb clause. More examples are given below. - Start when you are ready. (Adverb clause - when you are ready) - Will you wait till I return? (Adverb clause - till I return) - If you make a promise, you must keep it. (Adverb clause - if you make a promise) - I shall remain where I am. (Adverb clause - where I am) - Just as he entered the room the clock struck twelve. (Adverb clause - just as he entered the room) - He finished first though he started late. (Adverb clause - though he started late) As the Queen is old, people worry about her health, but she is rarely sick. However, she is leaving more duties to the younger members of the Royal Family, particularly to Prince Charles, who will become King when she dies. mm, XXX give the juice to the baby. Willie Nelson was born in Abbott, Texas on April 29, 1933, during the Great Depression to Myrle Marie (n e Greenhaw) and Ira Doyle Nelson. He was born on April 29, 1933, but his birth was recorded by doctor F. D. Sims on April 30. He was named Willie by his cousin Mildred, who also chose Hugh as his middle name, in honor of her recently deceased younger brother. Nelson's ancestry includes English, Irish, and Cherokee. His parents moved from Arkansas in 1929, to look for work. Nelson's grandfather, William, worked as a blacksmith, while his father worked as a mechanic. His mother left soon after he was born, and his father remarried and also moved away, leaving Willie and his sister Bobbie to be raised by their grandparents. The Nelsons, who taught singing back in Arkansas, started their grandchildren in music. Nelson's grandfather bought him a guitar when he was six, and taught him a few chords, and with his sister Bobbie, he sang gospel songs in the local church. He wrote his first song at age seven, and when he was nine, played guitar for the local band Bohemian Polka. During the summer, the family picked cotton along with other citizens of Abbott. Nelson didn't like picking cotton, so he earned money by singing in dance halls, taverns, and honky tonks from age thirteen, and continuing through high school. Nelson's musical influences were Hank Williams, Bob Wills, Lefty Frizzell, Ray Price, Ernest Tubb, Hank Snow, Django Reinhardt, Frank Sinatra and Louis Armstrong. [Nina puts a wooden girl on a swing.] Title: Getting an iOS 7 UDID Without a Computer (No iTunes and No Xcode) Introduction: The Unique Device Identifier (UDID) is a string of characters that is unique to every Apple device. It can be used by developers to test apps on specific devices before they are released on the App Store. If you want to get your iOS 7 device's UDID without using a computer, this tutorial will guide you through the process. This method does not require iTunes or Xcode, making it accessible for anyone with just their iPhone or iPad. Step 1: Prepare Your iOS Device Before starting, ensure your iOS device has internet connectivity and is updated to the latest version of iOS 7. Having a stable network connection is crucial as we will access online tools throughout the process. An up-to-date operating system guarantees compatibility with these external services. Key Tip: Make sure your device's battery level is sufficient for browsing and data transferring tasks. Step 2: Open Safari Browser Launch the built-in Safari browser on your iOS device. While there are other third-party browsers available, using Safari ensures maximum compatibility with our purpose here - retrieving the UDID. Step 3: Visit an Online UDID Tool In the address bar at the top, type one of the following URLs to visit websites offering free UDID extraction service: * <https://get.udid.io/> * <http://www.whatsmyudid.com> * <http://www.iphoneudiddownload.com/new-method-find-your-ipad-iphone-unique-device-identifier-udid/> These sites provide easy ways to extract your UDID directly from your iOS device via its web interface. Step 4: Authorize Web Clips Access Once you land on any of these pages, you might encounter prompts asking permission to create a 'Web clip'. Grant this authorization since these shortcuts enable direct interaction between the website and your device settings, facilitating the UDID fetching procedure. Step 5: Generate & Copy UDID After granting necessary permissions, you should see clear instructions on how to generate and copy your UDID within the webpage itself. Generally, tapping the 'Generate UDID' button initiates a configuration profile download onto your device. Install this temporary profile when prompted; doing so allows the site to read your UDID. After installation, return to the same page where you initially granted permission for the webclip. You should now find your complete UDID displayed on the screen. Long press over the revealed UDID until a 'Copy' option appears. Select this to save your UDID temporarily into the device memory. Guideline: Remember, never share your UDID publicly unless absolutely required, such as during beta testing phases under NDA conditions. Conclusion: With these steps completed successfully, you have obtained your iOS 7 device's UDID without needing a computer, iTunes, or Xcode! Keep your UDID safe and handy whenever needed for development purposes. You just I guess put the shapes where you want them to go. One difference between Neptune and Uranus is the level of meteorological activity that has been observed. When the Voyager spacecraft flew by Uranus in 1986, the winds on that planet were observed to be mild. When Voyager flew by Neptune in 1989, powerful weather events were observed. The weather of Neptune has extremely active storm systems. It's atmosphere has the highest wind speeds in the Solar System, thought to be powered by internal heat flow. Regular winds in the equatorial region have speeds of around 1,200 km/h (750 mph). Winds in storm systems can reach up to 2,100 km/h, near-supersonic speeds. Soil Studies: Soil Particle Sizes Objective: Students will learn soil size classifications (clay, silt, sand) and their effects on soil composition. Grade level: Elementary - Intermediate Time: First Activity - 30-45 minutes Second Activity - 30-40 minutes Season: Fall and spring Materials: For each student - 1 jar and a card or a piece of heavy paper For each group - 2 small tin cans (8-12 oz.) with one end open and the other end with many small holes in it Soil particles vary greatly in size, and soil scientists classify soil particles into sand, silt, and clay. Starting with the finest, clay particles are smaller than 0.002 mm in diameter. Some clay particles are so small that ordinary microscopes do not show them. Silt particles are from 0.002 to 0.05 mm in diameter. Sand ranges from 0.05 to 2.0 mm. Particles larger than 2.0 mm are called gravel or stones. Most soils contain a mixture of sand, silt and clay in different proportions. The size of soil particles is important. The amount of open space between the particles influences how easily water moves through a soil and how much water the soil will hold. Too much clay, in proportion to silt and sand, causes a soil to take in water very slowly. Such a soil gives up its water to plants slowly. These soils are sticky when wet. Loam and silt loam refer to soils that have a favorable proportion of sand, silt, and clay. A silt loam, for example, contains no more than 50% sand or more than 27% clay. The rest is silt. First Activity Description Have students collect some soil from their yards or from the school yard and fill a good sized jar about 1/2 to 2/3 full with it. When they get back to school fill the rest of the jar with water, replace the cover (tightly) and shake it vigorously. Put the jars on a table and have the students label theirs. Allow the jars to stand for a few days until all the particles settle out. Then hold a card or heavy piece of paper against the side of the jar and draw a diagram showing the different layers. Label each layer (clay, silt, sand). What percentages of each layer did you find? Which soils do you think would be better for plant growth? How do the Adirondack soils in your jar compare with the soils in the picture? Why is there a difference? Size of soil particles is important for other reasons, too. It affects the ease of working the soil, what crops can be grown, and the efficiency of certain fertilizers. Sandy soils that have no fine clay or silt particles filling the pore space cannot hold as much moisture since there is no surface area for the water to cling to and the pores are so large that the weight of the water causes much of it to run down and out of the soil. For this reason, medium and coarse sandy soils, low in clay are known as droughty soils. Crops cannot live long in them without very frequent rains. When fine soil particles fill the large pore spaces, the soil can hold more water for plants because there is more surface area for water to cling to. And since the size of the pores is reduced, the weight of the water is less and it does not run out of the soil so readily. Second Activity Description Have the students work in groups for this. Fill two cans with marbles and add sand to one can, filling up the spaces between the marbles (tap the can gently on the table while doing this to be sure all the pore spaces are filled). Now add a specific amount of water (50ml, 100ml, etc.) to each can and let it percolate through the soil. Collect and measure the remaining water to compare it with the original amount. Also, time them from when the first drop comes out of the bottom of the can till the last comes out. Which soil held more water in it? Which took more time to empty out? Which do you think would be better for plant growth? Way better than the ones I bought you buddy.
